sourceMovie.title: The Killing of a Sacred Deer
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto The Killing of a Sacred Deer (2017) as a drama theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The drama and thriller blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Gone Girl
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Killing of a Sacred Deer clicked for you, Gone Girl (2014) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama and thriller, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after The Killing of a Sacred Deer?
faq.answer: Try Gone Girl, Pacific Heights, Suddenly, Last Summer—each shares DNA with The Killing of a Sacred Deer while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is The Killing of a Sacred Deer?
faq.answer: The Killing of a Sacred Deer (2017) is best described as Drama, Thriller, Mystery. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is The Killing of a Sacred Deer worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.0/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this drama lane.
